title: ranunculus pulchellus var. membranaceus
intro: Ranunculus pulchellus var. membranaceus, also known as small-flowered buttercup, is a perennial plant belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. Its health benefits include soothing skin irritations, reducing swelling, and promoting wound healing. It can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or tincture, often used topically to treat various skin conditions and injuries.
